html:
Daily Economic Briefing

Tuesday, 14 July 2026

MOVE 66.79 ElevatedSOFR 3.55% StableNFCI −0.515 LoosePPI 08:30 ET
📊 Liquidity & Stress Metrics — The "Gnosis" Pulse
MetricValueMarket Context
MOVE Index66.79Latest verified close (10 Jul). Treasury volatility remains elevated enough that inflation surprises can transmit quickly into ES through real yields and term premium.
SOFR Rate3.55%New York Fed reference rate for 10 Jul. Secured overnight funding is orderly; there is no current plumbing-stress signal.
NFCI−0.515Latest reading, week ending 3 Jul. A negative NFCI means broad financial conditions remain looser than their historical average; the next update is Wednesday.
Gnosis Interpretation
Funding and broad conditions remain supportive, but the rates-volatility backdrop is not benign. Treat PPI as the first directional test; calm SOFR and loose NFCI support risk appetite only if yields absorb the inflation signal.
📅 Economic Calendar: 2026-07-14
Investing.com's U.S. two-/three-star calendar was used only for discovery/cross-checking. Selected releases and rates events were checked against official agency schedules or issuer calendars where available.
EventETGMT+8Notes
Producer Price Index, June08:3020:30BLS inflation release. Watch core services and goods details alongside the headline: a hot print can lift the front end and pressure duration-sensitive equity multiples.
Fed Chair Kevin Warsh, House testimony10:0022:00Policy communication follows the inflation print. Focus on any reaction-function language, balance-sheet guidance, and whether the Chair validates or resists the rates move.
10-Year Treasury Note Auction13:0001:00 (+1)Rates-demand checkpoint after PPI. Tail, bid-to-cover, and dealer take-up matter more for ES than the auction's standalone headline.
Monthly Treasury Statement, June14:0002:00 (+1)Federal budget data. Lower immediate impulse than PPI, but receipts, outlays, and deficit headlines can shape the supply narrative.
🏛️ Geopolitical & Fed Watch
Inflation-to-Rates Sequence
PPI, Chair testimony, and the 10-year auction create a single rates-risk chain. A higher-than-expected inflation result that is followed by weak long-note demand is the adverse combination for equity duration.
Energy & Market Structure
No weekly EIA petroleum-status report is scheduled today; it remains a Wednesday risk. The EIA STEO was released 7 Jul, not today. Friday's monthly options expiration is the next known structure date, so dealer positioning can become more influential after the macro sequence clears.
📈 Trading Strategy Note (ES Futures)
Base case: Let the first PPI/yields reaction establish the tradeable regime. If yields settle after the release, loose financial conditions favor buying accepted pullbacks rather than chasing the initial headline move.
Key windows: 08:30 ET is the main impulse; reassess at 10:00 ET for policy language and 13:00 ET for 10-year demand. Avoid carrying a conviction trade through each unpriced rates event.
Invalidation: If PPI is hot, the Chair does not offset the hawkish read, and the 10-year tails, reduce size and favor responsive selling. Do not let orderly SOFR or loose NFCI override a simultaneous MOVE-and-yields surge.
